MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF STEPHANIE DENNY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, or motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is GRANED and the trial date is continued to August 20, 2018. Ms. Denny has shown good cause for leave to file her proposed new complaint, granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage in a case, and defendants have not identified any legal prejudice they would suffer if the motion was granted other than their diminished ability to properly respond to the new material due to the imminence of the current trial date. That asserted prejudice can be remedied by a continuance of the trial. While Ms. Denny was arguably less than fully diligent in seeking to allege her new claims and allegations and add a new defendant, in the absence of irremediable prejudice to defendants, the arguable lack of diligence is an insufficient basis to overcome California's strong policy favoring the granting of the motion. This strong policy is especially applicable here given that Ms. Denny proposes to represent a class of similarly situated persons, whose interests would very possibly be adversely affected by denial of this motion. Thus, the public interest underlying the availability of class actions also favors the granting of this motion. The trial date is continued to August 20, 2018 at 9:30am in department 206 with all discovery and other dates triggered off the new trial date. In light of the granting of this motion, the parties are encouraged to consider whether it is appropriate to modify the recently entered bifurcation order and/or seek complex designation for this case. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
